title: P0420 Catalyst Efficiency Below Threshold Bank 1 — Diagnostic Playbook
description: Decision tree for P0420 (catalytic converter efficiency below threshold). Distinguishes between a truly failed catalytic converter, a bad downstream O2 sensor, and other causes that mimic catalyst failure.
common_symptoms: no driveability symptoms in most cases
physical_solution: P0420 does NOT automatically mean the converter needs replacement. Diagnose downstream O2 sensor health, check for rich-running conditions, and confirm with waveform comparison before condemning converter.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Full — O2 sensor voltage PIDs fully accessible via Mode $01. Mode $06 catalyst test results accessible on all OBD-II. No OEM tool needed for diagnosis.
preconditions: Drive at highway speeds to run catalyst monitor
No active misfires or rich-running conditions

procedure_steps.description: ⚠️ DO NOT REPLACE CONVERTER IMMEDIATELY. P0420 has a false-positive rate >30%. Always diagnose before condemning a $500-$2000 part.

Read upstream O2 (pre-cat) and downstream O2 (post-cat): Mode $01 PIDs $14/$15 (O2 bank 1 sensor 1/2). UPSTREAM should switch rapidly 0.1-0.9V. DOWNSTREAM on a GOOD catalyst should stay steady near 0.6-0.7V (cat is buffering).

DOWNSTREAM O2 WAVEFORM: If downstream O2 is switching rapidly (mimicking upstream) = catalyst not storing oxygen = converter failed OR downstream O2 sensor lazy/stuck-switching (false P0420). Compare switching frequency: upstream fast switching OK, downstream slow/flat = good cat.

Read Mode $06 catalyst monitor results. ELM327: AT SH 7DF, 06 21. Returns catalyst efficiency test value vs min/max threshold. Value near threshold = marginal cat. Well below threshold = strong converter failure signal.

CHECK FUEL TRIMS: Mode $01 PID $07 (LTFT). If LTFT is positive lean (+10%+): false P0420 possible — lean O2 bias can mimic catalyst failure. Fix lean condition first, clear code, re-run catalyst monitor. Rich conditions also damage converter.

DOWNSTREAM SENSOR TEST: Tap on downstream O2 sensor connector (engine running). If P0420 clears temporarily or downstream O2 pattern improves = loose/faulty sensor connection causing false signal. Replace downstream sensor (much cheaper than converter).

CONVERTER PHYSICAL CHECK: Shake converter — rattling metalite substrate = converter internally damaged. Tap with rubber mallet — hollow vs solid sound difference. Infrared temp gun: converter should be 50-150°F hotter on outlet than inlet when functioning. Equal temps = no catalytic activity.

After repair: clear P0420. Complete catalyst monitor drive cycle (cold start → 20 min highway at 55-60mph → idle 5 min). Catalyst monitor must run to READY. If P0420 does not return after 3 drive cycles: repair successful.

oem_notes.Toyota/Lexus: Toyota P0420 has high false-positive rate from downstream sensor (#2) failure. Toyota sensor uses narrowband O2 — $80-150 to replace vs $800+ for OEM converter. Always test sensor first. Toyota OEM converters are durable — P0420 before 150k miles often = sensor issue.
oem_notes.Honda/Acura: Honda P0420 often from oil burning fouling the converter (K-series valve stem seals). If oil consumption present: fix burning before replacing converter or new converter will fail within 20k miles.
oem_notes.Nissan/Infiniti: VQ35: P0420 + P0430 common together (both banks). May indicate fuel trim issue affecting both banks. Check MAF and IACV first. Nissan converters generally durable.
oem_notes.Ford: EcoBoost: P0420 from rich conditions (injector leaking). 2.5L Duratec: downstream O2 sensor failures common. Ford recommends verifying fuel trim and misfire history before converter replacement.
oem_notes.GM: V8 engines: P0420 + P0430 together with AFM issues. Fix AFM lifter problem first — oil in exhaust destroys converter. 3.6L: check cam follower before condemning converter.
oem_notes.BMW: BMW P0420 often from N54/N55 injector leaking rich. Rich conditions poison catalyst quickly. Fix injector first. BMW OEM converters expensive — verify with Mode $06 before ordering.
oem_notes.VW/Audi: EA888: P0420 from PCV failure causing oil burning. Fix PCV first. TDI: SCR/DPF system different — P0420 equivalent codes are emissions system faults requiring DEF/AdBlue check.
oem_notes.Stellantis: Hemi 5.7: P0420 + P0430 from MDS-related oil burning. Fix MDS first. Pentastar 3.6L cam follower failures cause exhaust contamination — address mechanical cause before converter.
oem_notes.Hyundai/Kia: Theta II: P0420 well documented as downstream O2 sensor failure on 2014-2019 models. GDS2 shows O2 sensor adaptation — high adaptation = sensor at end of life. Replace sensor first (common fix under $150).
